description_long: This is a large, weathered ceramic vase with a rounded body and a single, thick handle attached at the upper rim and shoulder. The surface is marked by horizontal incised lines and subtle, irregular texturing, giving it an aged, rustic appearance. The rim is uneven and slightly flared, with a wide opening suitable for holding water or decorative arrangements. The handle is robust and curves gracefully from the rim to the body, showing signs of wear and minor chipping. The coloration is predominantly a faded grayish-white with darker patches and some greenish discoloration near the base, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The overall form is utilitarian yet decorative, reminiscent of traditional Mediterranean or ancient urban pottery.
description: A large, weathered ceramic vase with a single thick handle, incised horizontal lines, and a faded grayish-white surface with signs of age and outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: The vase is seen from a front angle, highlighting the wide, uneven rim and the body’s incised lines, with the handle partially visible on the right.
description_view_1: This side view clearly displays the full handle, showing its attachment points and curvature, as well as the vase’s rounded profile and surface texture.
description_view_2: The back view emphasizes the handle’s thickness and attachment, with the rim’s irregularity and the vase’s overall weathered surface more apparent.
description_view_3: Another side view, showing the opposite side of the handle and the vase’s broad, gently tapering body with visible incised lines and surface wear.
